leagueBriefing: Conference tournament play across women's college basketball produced blowouts, dramatic comebacks and a few buzzer-beater finishes as Championship Week intensified. Kentucky delivered the most dominant win of the day in the SEC Tournament, while Indiana erased a 20-point deficit in the Big Ten and Auburn stole a thriller in the final seconds against Texas A&M. Across the ACC, Big 12 and Big Ten tournaments, several double-digit seeds advanced as the postseason chaos of March officially began.

games.headline: Kentucky explodes in second half to rout Arkansas in SEC Tournament opener
games.brief: Kentucky blew the game open with an 18-2 run after halftime and rolled to the largest SEC Tournament win in program history, overwhelming Arkansas with balanced scoring and relentless rebounding.
bullets: Clara Strack posted 20 points, 13 rebounds and three blocks.
Amelia Hassett hit six 3-pointers and scored 18 points.
Kentucky opened the second half with an 18-2 run to seize control.
The Wildcats won 94-64 — the largest SEC Tournament victory in program history.

games.headline: Miami survives Stanford rally, wins ACC Tournament opener in overtime
games.brief: Miami appeared in control before Stanford stormed back late, but the Hurricanes regrouped in overtime behind Amarachi Kimpson and Ra Shaya Kyle to secure the tournament win.
bullets: Ra Shaya Kyle recorded 25 points and 11 rebounds.
Amarachi Kimpson scored 20 points including key overtime baskets.
Stanford erased a 13-point deficit to force overtime.
Miami pulled away in OT for an 83-76 win.

games.headline: Kansas State rains tournament-record threes in Big 12 rout of Cincinnati
games.brief: Kansas State turned the game into a three-point showcase, tying a program record and setting a Big 12 Tournament mark with a barrage from deep against Cincinnati.
bullets: Kansas State hit 17 three-pointers, a Big 12 Tournament record.
Jordan Speiser and Taryn Sides scored 20 points each.
Nastja Claessens added 18 points and five 3-pointers.
Wildcats dominated 91-66 to advance.

games.headline: Florida cruises past Mississippi State behind McGill and O’Neal
games.brief: Florida controlled the game from start to finish as Liv McGill and Me’Arah O’Neal powered the Gators to their largest SEC Tournament victory.
bullets: Liv McGill posted 22 points, 10 assists and seven rebounds.
Me’Arah O’Neal added 22 points including three second-quarter threes.
Florida never trailed in the 86-68 victory.
Mississippi State dropped its fifth straight game.

games.headline: Indiana storms back from 20 down to stun Nebraska in Big Ten Tournament
games.brief: Indiana pulled off one of the most dramatic comebacks of the day, erasing a massive deficit and finishing strong in the closing minutes to defeat Nebraska.
bullets: Indiana rallied from a 20-point second-quarter deficit.
Shay Ciezki scored 22 points with seven assists.
Nevaeh Caffey scored nine of her 13 points in the fourth quarter.
The Hoosiers won 72-69 to extend their late-season surge.

games.headline: Auburn edges Texas A&M with late layup in SEC thriller
games.brief: Auburn grabbed the lead with just seconds left as Khady Leye drove for the decisive basket, sealing a dramatic one-point win in the SEC Tournament.
bullets: Khady Leye scored the go-ahead layup with 5.2 seconds remaining.
Leye finished with 11 points and 15 rebounds.
Ny’Ceara Pryor led Texas A&M with 25 points.
Auburn held on for a 50-49 win.

games.headline: Illinois pulls away from Wisconsin in Big Ten opener
games.brief: Illinois used a big second-half run and strong free-throw shooting to put away Wisconsin and move forward in the Big Ten Tournament.
bullets: Berry Wallace scored 22 points and Destiny Jackson added 21.
Illinois outscored Wisconsin 24-10 at the free-throw line.
The Illini used a 16-3 run in the third quarter to take control.
Illinois won 82-70.

games.headline: Georgia Tech controls Florida State in ACC Tournament opener
games.brief: Georgia Tech led nearly the entire game and sealed the victory with a late run to eliminate Florida State in the ACC Tournament.
bullets: La’Nya Foster scored 18 points with eight rebounds.
Erica Moon added 17 points.
Georgia Tech closed with a 9-2 run in the fourth quarter.
Yellow Jackets won 72-60.

games.headline: BYU holds off Houston to advance in Big 12 Tournament
games.brief: BYU built a steady lead and survived a late push from Houston to secure the program’s first Big 12 Tournament victory.
bullets: Olivia Hamlin led BYU with 16 points.
Delaney Gibb and Lara Rohkohl added 15 points each.
BYU shot 47% compared to Houston’s 31%.
Cougars won 76-66.

games.headline: Arizona State edges Arizona in tense rivalry matchup
games.brief: A tight rivalry game came down to late jumpers as Arizona State held off Arizona in a defensive battle at the Big 12 Tournament.
bullets: Heloisa Carrera scored 16 points including the go-ahead jumper.
Arizona State took the lead for good late in the fourth quarter.
Arizona’s final possession failed to produce a tying shot.
Sun Devils won 54-51.

games.headline: Oregon rolls past Purdue in Big Ten Tournament opener
games.brief: Oregon built a huge halftime lead and never looked back, advancing comfortably past Purdue in Indianapolis.
bullets: Katie Fiso scored 20 points for Oregon.
Ehis Etute added 16 points and 12 rebounds.
Ducks led 47-24 at halftime.
Oregon secured an 82-64 victory.

games.headline: Kansas defense overwhelms UCF in Big 12 opener
games.brief: Kansas used a dominant defensive performance and a decisive scoring run to eliminate UCF in the first round of the Big 12 Tournament.
bullets: Jaliya Davis posted a double-double with 10 points and 11 rebounds.
Kansas held UCF to just 35 points.
UCF shot 25% overall and 11% from three.
Jayhawks won 56-35.
